purpose: Channel-2 feedback arm (language -> prediction, not language -> gamma). Base is IDENTICAL to the frozen static/hard-constraint baseline (safe_panda_core_fixed_g015): gamma stays fixed at 0.15 throughout, prediction_mode starts static, cbf_slack_weight 0. The ONLY difference from the baseline is a scripted mid-episode switch of prediction_mode from static to velocity_tube at the frozen per-instance feedback time. Directly comparable to safe_panda_core_scripted_feedback_v1 (which switches gamma instead, on the same base, same timing, and was proven outcome-inert: 150/150 concordant, p=1.0) -- this arm isolates whether routing feedback into the prediction channel instead of the gamma channel rescues the hard-constraint baseline.
notes: gamma is NEVER touched by this manifest; only prediction_mode changes, at the same frozen feedback_intervention_time_s used by the scripted gamma arm, so timing is held constant across both channel comparisons.

The constant-velocity observer already tracks obstacle velocity from every ZOH measurement regardless of prediction_mode, so the switch is never cold-started -- it inherits whatever estimate has accumulated by the feedback time.
No slack, no dead-time margin: this arm tests the prediction channel in isolation against the hard-constraint baseline, mirroring the scripted gamma arm's isolation of the gamma channel.
